_notes: Researcher: people_company line (selector types: name, company). Covers PERSON identification/public-records, COMPANY/org recon, and BUSINESS REGISTRIES. Verified maintained status + endpoints/auth via WebSearch/WebFetch mid-2026. Honest gap: real person-identification (name -> address/relatives/phone) is overwhelmingly paid people-search or web-search; there is NO good free structured tool for it. Already-implemented name tools live in src/tools/name_tools.py.

best.name: Wikidata
best.run: GET https://www.wikidata.org/w/api.php?action=wbsearchentities&search={selector}&language=en&format=json  (then wbgetentities for claims P106 occupation / P108 employer / P569 DOB / P27 citizenship)
best.notes: Best STRUCTURED person source but only covers notable/public figures. Already wired in name_tools.py (WikidataTool). Pulls occupation/employer/DOB/citizenship + Wikipedia sitelink.

best.name: Wikipedia
best.notes: Biographical context for notable persons only. Already wired (WikipediaSearchTool).

best.name: Gravatar profile check
best.run: GET https://en.gravatar.com/{md5(email)}.json  (email candidates derived from name)
best.notes: Confirms an email/name pair when a Gravatar exists; yields linked accounts. Already wired (GravatarTool). Pivots off name->email guesses, low hit rate but high confidence on hit.

runner_up.name: Name->username (GitHub/GitLab)
runner_up.run: name -> username permutations -> GET api.github.com/users/{u} + gitlab.com/api/v4/users?username={u}
runner_up.notes: Already wired (UsernameFromNameTool). Bridges name -> username/url for the username line. Confirms when GitHub display name matches.

capabilities.gaps: HONEST GAP: there is NO good free/structured tool for real person identification of non-notable people (name -> address, relatives, phone, age, prior addresses). That capability is owned by (a) the WEB-SEARCH line, and (b) PAID people-search/identity APIs (Pipl, OSINT Industries, Predicta Search, TruePeopleSearch, BeenVerified, Spokeo). All are excluded here as paid-only/web-only. hibp_name_search (name_tools.py) does NOT do any breach lookup -- it only generates email permutations; the only real breach source is HIBP API v3 (keyed, ~$3.95/mo, in the email/breach bucket, not here).

best.name: theHarvester
best.install: pipx install theHarvester   (Python 3.11+; alt: pip install -r requirements/base.txt from repo)
best.auth: none for free sources; some -b sources (shodan, hunter, securityTrails, github-code, etc.) need API keys in api-keys.yaml
best.maintained: active (laramies; v4.11.x, repo indexed Mar 2026 -- most actively maintained recon CLI)
best.notes: BEST org-recon tool. Takes a DOMAIN (not a free-text company name) -> emails, subdomains, hosts, IPs, sometimes employee names. For a company SELECTOR, first resolve company -> primary domain (via Wikidata P856 official website, or registry below), then run theHarvester on that domain. JSON output via -f. Needs the CLI-collector runner class (audit S4). Pin version; isolate (some sources rate-limit).

runner_up.name: Hunter.io Domain Search
runner_up.notes: Cleanest domain -> employee email+name+role API. Free tier ~25-50 searches/mo (keyed). Excellent for company people-discovery but key-gated, so runner-up to the keyless theHarvester.

capabilities.gaps: Org recon is domain-anchored, so a free-text company NAME must first be resolved to a domain (use Wikidata official-website P856, or the registry tools below which sometimes carry a website field). Employee enumeration with names/titles at scale is mostly LinkedIn (no compliant free API) or paid (Hunter, RocketReach, Apollo, Clearbit).

best.name: GLEIF LEI API v1
best.maintained: active (GLEIF foundation, live; free, no registration)
best.notes: GLOBAL, FREE, no key. Company name -> LEI code, registered legal name, HQ + legal address, jurisdiction, registration status, parent/child (relationship records via /lei-records/{lei}/direct-parent etc.). JSON:API format (data[].attributes.entity). Verified endpoint live mid-2026. Best free global registry pivot.

best.name: SEC EDGAR (full-text + submissions)
best.run: name->CIK: GET https://www.sec.gov/cgi-bin/browse-edgar?action=getcompany&company={selector}&type=10-K&output=atom  OR  GET https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22{selector}%22 ; then submissions: GET https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{10-digit-zero-padded}.json ; ticker map: GET https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json
best.auth: none (REQUIRES descriptive User-Agent header, e.g. 'name email'; rate ~10 req/s)
best.maintained: active (US SEC, live JSON APIs on data.sec.gov + efts.sec.gov)
best.notes: US public companies only. Company name -> CIK -> filings, officers/insiders, business address, former names, tickers. efts.sec.gov full-text search covers electronic filings since 2001. No key; MUST send a real User-Agent or you get 403. Great for officer/exec names of US public cos.

best.name: UK Companies House API
best.auth: api_key (free; HTTP Basic, API key as username, blank password)
best.notes: UK companies. Company name -> company number, status, registered address, incorporation date, officers/directors (NAMES + partial DOB + addresses), PSC (beneficial owners), filing history. Free key, 600 req / 5 min. Best free source for director/UBO NAMES -> strong people pivot. Needs key in credential vault.

runner_up.name: Wikidata (company facts)
runner_up.run: wbsearchentities then wbgetentities: P856 official website, P159 HQ, P1278/P3548 LEI, P1320 OpenCorporates ID, P749 parent org
runner_up.notes: Already wired (WikidataTool). Useful glue: resolves company NAME -> official website/domain (feeds theHarvester) and -> LEI / OpenCorporates ID / parent org. Notable companies only.

capabilities.gaps: No single free GLOBAL company-by-name registry beyond GLEIF (which is LEI-scoped, so smaller private firms without an LEI are missing). OpenCorporates -- the broadest aggregator (200M+ companies, 140+ jurisdictions) -- is now PAID-ONLY (see excluded). Country registries outside US/UK are fragmented (each jurisdiction has its own site, mostly no clean API). For non-US/UK/LEI'd companies, coverage is a real gap -> falls to web search.

excluded.reason: PAID-ONLY as of 2026. No free/no-key automation tier remains; cheapest self-serve API plan 'Essentials' is GBP 2,250/yr (<=500 calls/mo, 200/day), and a failed/empty search still consumes a call (verified zephira.ai pricing analysis, Jun 2026). Was historically the best global company aggregator (200M+ cos), but excluded as paid-with-no-usable-free-tier. Wikidata P1320 still maps name->OpenCorporates ID if a user has a key.
Duplicate of GLEIF API. ext_0530_lei_search in tool_buckets is the search.gleif.org WEB UI (web_interface / manual). Superseded by the api.gleif.org JSON API picked above.

ext_0531 openownership.org -- beneficial-ownership register, but a manual website/bulk-data download, not a clean per-query lookup API. Bulk-data/manual only; not wireable as a per-selector collector now.

Paid identity/people-search APIs (Pipl, OSINT Industries, Predicta Search). These are the real 'last mile' for name->identity per the audit, but all paid with no free tier. Out of scope for the free structured line.

US consumer people-search sites (name->address/relatives/phone/age). Manual-website or paid, anti-bot, ToS-restricted, no clean free API. Person identification of non-notable people belongs to the WEB-SEARCH line, not the structured line.

NOT a breach tool despite its name (in name_tools.py). It performs NO breach/HIBP lookup -- it only generates name->email permutations (and a no-op pwnedpasswords range call). Listed here only to flag the mislabel; real breach coverage = HIBP API v3 (keyed) in the email bucket. Keep as an email-candidate generator, not a person-id source.

Highest-value source for company employees/titles but has no compliant free API; scraping is ToS-violating and heavily anti-bot. Excluded from the structured line; partial coverage only via paid (Apollo, RocketReach) or web search.
